name: Trans American Aquaculture, Inc
business: Trans American Aquaculture, Inc. provides premium quality, sustainably raised, farmed Pacific white shrimp, 100% free of antibiotics and hormones, to the U.S. domestic seafood market. The company also sells broodstock for breeding purposes to foreign producers. Its main product is Pacific White Leg Shrimp, both head-on and headless/shell-on, focusing on large shrimp (over 28 grams).

exposures.sentence: We provide extra-large farm-raised Pacific white shrimp, 100% free of antibiotics and hormones, to the U.S. domestic seafood market.

catalysts.event: Plan confirmation hearing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
catalysts.sentence: The plan confirmation hearing is scheduled for August 18, 2025, at which time, we will present the re-organization plan for the company.

tamClaims.claim: The total global value for shrimp trade in 2022 was $24B USD.
tamClaims.sentence: The total global value for shrimp trade in 2022 was $24B USD.

tamClaims.claim: The total global production of farm raised shrimp in 2022 was slightly over 4.0 MMT or 8.8 billion pounds.
tamClaims.sentence: The total global production of farm raised shrimp in 2022 was slightly over 4.0 MMT or 8.8 billion pounds.

tamClaims.claim: The United States is the second largest import market and second largest consumer market for shrimp in the world having consumed 1.6B lbs. of shrimp in 2021.
tamClaims.sentence: The United States is the second largest import market and second largest consumer market for shrimp in the world having consumed 1.6B lbs. of shrimp in 2021.

tamClaims.claim: Annual production growth estimated to be 6.72% CARG through 2028.
tamClaims.sentence: The demand and production of farm raised shrimp is at an all-time high with annual production growth estimated to be 6.72% CARG (compounded annual growth rate) through 2028.
